nodes.name: The Hobbit
nodes.summary:
"The Hobbit" is the tenth episode of South Park's 17th season, aired on December 11, 2013. It features Wendy Testaburger's campaign on media influence on body image and Kanye West's attempt to prove Kim Kardashian is not a hobbit.

nodes.name: Dog with a Blog
nodes.summary: Stan, the dog, secretly blogs, talks, and is close to Avery. He hates noisy pomeranians, loves poodles, and has a toy monkey named Robert. His secret is revealed in 'The Kids Find Out Stan Blogs'.

Spider-Man (1967 TV series) is the original animated TV series based on the Spider-Man comic book series.
The Spider-Man comic book series was created by writer Stan Lee, on which the Spider-Man (1967 TV series) is based.

Grantray-Lawrence Animation produced the first season of Spider-Man (1967 TV series).
Krantz Films produced the second and third seasons of Spider-Man (1967 TV series).
Paul Soles starred as the voice of Peter Parker in the Spider-Man (1967 TV series).

nodes.name: Barry Robinson
nodes.summary:
Barry Robinson's normal voice is provided by Eddie Kaye Thomas.
Barry Robinson's evil voice is provided by Craig Ferguson.
Barry Robinson is Steve's morbidly obese friend.
Stan often yells insults at Barry Robinson about his weight without knowing why.
Barry Robinson, like Snot, can speak Klingon and seems to read Quenya.
Barry Robinson apparently has a fetish for Miss Piggy.
When deranged, Barry Robinson speaks in a low-pitched voice like Gary Oldman.
In one episode, deranged Barry Robinson frames Steve for destroying Stan's commemorative plates.
Deranged Barry Robinson attempts to replace Steve within Stan's immediate family.
Barry Robinson tries to eliminate anyone who might come between him and Stan during his madness.
Barry Robinson helps Snot and Toshi beat up a drugged Steve and urges them to kill Steve's family.
Barry Robinson collaborates with Toshi and Snot to beat up drugged Steve and suggests killing Steve's family.
